name: Windows Registry Key Deletion
description: The removal of a registry key within the Windows operating system. Adversaries often delete registry keys to clean up configuration changes, remove persistence mechanisms (after a job is done), or hide evidence of intrusion.

event_logs.description: Object Deleted; logs when a registry key is deleted.
Registry Key Handle Closed; captures when a handle to a registry key is closed (often required to correlate with 4660 to identify the specific object).

sysmon.description: Registry Key Deleted; logs when a registry key is removed.
Registry Value Deleted; captures removal of specific registry values.

capabilities: Monitor registry deletions for suspicious behavior.
Correlate deletions with process execution history.
